name: Comment Thread
description: Inline or sidebar comment discussions with reply threading, @mentions, reactions, and resolve functionality.

sidebar.description: Full thread display in aside panel

inline.description: Compact thread attached to text selection

slots.comments: List of comment messages with avatar, author, timestamp

visual_brief: Vertical thread of comment messages with an anchor line connecting to the commented content. Each comment shows a small avatar, author name in bold, timestamp in muted text, and the comment body. Replies indent slightly with a thin left border. A textarea input at the bottom allows new replies with a send button. A resolve button (checkmark icon) sits in the thread header. Resolved threads show a green checkmark and collapsed state. Sidebar preset renders as a right-side panel. Inline preset positions the thread as a popover near the anchor point.

responsive.mobile: Thread renders full-width as a bottom sheet. Avatar size reduces. Reply input is full-width. Thread collapses to show only the first comment with an expand button.
responsive.tablet: Sidebar preset renders as a right panel at 320px width. Inline preset uses a popover.
responsive.desktop: Standard sidebar or inline popover layout. Full avatars and comfortable spacing.

layout_hints.thread_role: The thread should feel attached to the referenced content first and like a discussion surface second. Anchor cues, indentation, and resolve state should preserve that relationship.
layout_hints.reply_hierarchy: Primary comment, replies, and thread actions should read in a clear top-to-bottom progression. Resolve controls should be easy to find without visually dominating the discussion.
layout_hints.inline_vs_sidebar: Inline threads should stay compact and context-tethered, while sidebar threads may breathe more and show a fuller reply composer.

motion.micro: Resolve state, hover actions, and reply affordances may fade or sharpen in gently as attention moves through the thread.
motion.transitions: Opening, collapsing, or resolving threads should feel calm and collaborative rather than abrupt or chatty.

keyboard: Thread messages, reply composer, and resolve actions should follow a predictable reading order.
Resolved and reopened states should remain clear to keyboard and assistive-technology users.
announcements: Author, timestamp, reply depth, and resolution state should be communicated semantically, not only through indentation or color.
